exclusion_note: Cherry Blossom / Pussy Willow / twig-based blossom exclusion LIFTED per Bret's confirmation. No permanent exclusions remain.

authored_emotional_profile.emotional_arc: From sorrow toward beauty; from loss toward tenderness
authored_emotional_profile.texture_archetype: Lush, voluminous, ruffled — fullest bloom in canon

species.notes: No closed-bud SKUs in current inventory — every Peony is full-bloom/peak. Quiet-grief register requires substitution (Ranunculus Bud, Tulip Closed Bud).

authored_emotional_profile.emotional_arc: From longing toward closeness; from memory toward presence

species.notes: Deep-red devotion register thin in stock — real inventory dominated by dusty rose/blush/coral/cream. Cultivar names in stock (Juliet, Ambridge, Cressida, Dutchess, Beatrix Potter, Westminster, Tamora, Emmanuelle, Princess, Heritage, Charmaine) usable directly in product copy.

species.notes: 52 SKUs — largest single species by count. Splits into Open/Full-Bloom (romantic, luxurious, moderate-raw) and Bud (delicate, ethereal, quiet — closest to canon's original grief framing).

authored_emotional_profile.emotional_arc: From uncertainty toward confidence; from ordinary toward extraordinary

species.notes: $2.80 flat — cheapest stem in inventory. Fully confirms original 'structured/architectural/wild' framing.

authored_emotional_profile.primary_emotion: Tender awakening, romantic devotion, fresh hope
authored_emotional_profile.emotional_arc: Toward opening — from closed bud (private, held) to full bloom (open, declared)
authored_emotional_profile.texture_archetype: Smooth, cupped, faintly waxy-satin petal; deeply fringed in Parrot variety

species.notes: 42 SKUs across 5 sub-registers: Closed Bud (quiet/Gossamer-adjacent), Double/Ruffled (romantic/moderate), Classic Open (fresh/cheerful), Parrot (dramatic/gothic — never cluster, max 2), Water Color (painterly/vintage/nostalgic, unique gradient coloring).

authored_emotional_profile.primary_emotion: Rustic warmth, natural abundance, quiet festivity
authored_emotional_profile.secondary_emotion: Delicate ethereality (white/soft) or vibrant celebration (crimson/burnt orange)
authored_emotional_profile.emotional_arc: Toward harvest — what remains after the bloom is gone, and finding richness in that
authored_emotional_profile.texture_archetype: Clustered round seed pods, not petaled

species.notes: Seed form, not a bloom — same functional slot as Viburnum Berry. Pairs with Amaranthus and Magnolia for harvest registers.

authored_emotional_profile.emotional_arc: Toward endurance — blooms through late winter/early spring, often through snow
authored_emotional_profile.texture_archetype: Nodding, cup-shaped bloom — quieter presence than a rose despite the nickname

species.notes: Botanically unrelated to Rosa despite 'Christmas Rose' nickname. Natural 'nodding' form reads restrained — useful for grief/quiet registers wanting a bloom, not a bud.

authored_emotional_profile.secondary_emotion: Bold cheer (Magenta Pink, Crimson Red SKUs only)
authored_emotional_profile.emotional_arc: Toward open cheerfulness, tempered by soft stock reality

species.notes: ⚠ Same pattern as Dahlia — real stock skews soft/romantic against Zinnia's typical bold reputation. Only 2 of 9 SKUs carry real vibrance.

authored_emotional_profile.emotional_arc: Toward stability — the visual floor of an arrangement

species.notes: Functions as base-layer coverage — controls how much grapevine structure is visible beneath focal blooms.

authored_emotional_profile.emotional_arc: Toward directional movement without floral drama
authored_emotional_profile.texture_archetype: Long cascading vine line — directional, not diffuse

species.notes: Not the 'calming' register typical of foliage — reads architectural/modern/dramatic. Your quiet, green answer to Amaranthus.

authored_emotional_profile.emotional_arc: Toward dimensional fullness without a focal statement
authored_emotional_profile.texture_archetype: Bush-form, denser/more three-dimensional than a spray

species.notes: Italian Ficus is the outlier — tropical/modern register, more contemporary-editorial than the rest of this group.

authored_emotional_profile.secondary_emotion: Elegance, graceful devotion; occasional dramatic exoticism (Gloria Lily, red)

authored_emotional_profile.texture_archetype: Large trumpet bloom, classic sympathy flower

authored_emotional_profile.emotional_arc: Toward gentle wonder
authored_emotional_profile.texture_archetype: Thin, papery petal, dark-centered in some variants

species.notes: Functionally overlaps with Anemone — treat as its soft-pastel cousin, same visual-punctuation role, gentler default register.

authored_emotional_profile.emotional_arc: Toward open freshness

species.notes: Only true BLOOM (not foliage) in Periwinkle Blue and Lavender Purple — fills a real gap for Sad-sector/peace-register briefs.

species.notes: Softer, less structured filler than Ranunculus or Wax Flower.

authored_emotional_profile.texture_archetype: Airy branch texture, fine clustered detail

species.notes: Deep Purple and Lavender Purple SKUs give airy texture in a cooler register most airy fillers (Wax Flower, Lavender) don't cover.
